mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate (4.48%) combined with persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL 317.685) signals sustained monetary tightening. Higher real rates compress equity valuations and increase Treasury yields as the market prices in prolonged restrictive policy. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL > 310, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% signals tight labor market conditions. If payroll employment (PAYEMS 159536) continues to grow, wage pressures persist, forcing the Fed to maintain elevated rates longer. This sustains upward pressure on Treasury yields as terminal rate expectations remain high.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100k
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 20 trading days when both UNRATE < 4.3% and PAYEMS growth > 100k, or interaction effect (joint signal) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST 1289.0) and retail sales (RSAFS 724609.0) indicate robust aggregate demand. Strong demand pressures inflation expectations and signals the Fed will keep rates elevated. Higher terminal rate expectations push 10-year Treasury yields upward over the 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite both HOUST > 1250 and RSAFS > 700000, or joint signal produces negative Rank IC < -0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO 101.9621) near trend suggests supply-side capacity is not constraining growth. Absent supply shocks, demand-driven inflation persists, requiring sustained monetary tightening and higher Treasury yields. This is an always-on mechanism reflecting structural supply conditions.
regime_hint: any regime with INDPRO near or above trend
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 60 trading days while INDPRO remains > 101.5, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: If federal funds rate crosses above 4.75%, the market reprices terminal rate expectations sharply upward, causing a discrete jump in 10-year Treasury yields. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when FEDFUNDS breaches the 4.75% level, signaling a more hawkish Fed stance than currently priced.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with hawkish surprise
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 5 trading days following a FEDFUNDS > 4.75% observation, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market softening and reduced wage pressure. The Fed may pivot toward easing, lowering terminal rate expectations and compressing 10-year Treasury yields downward. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it activates only in tightening cycles where labor deterioration triggers policy reversal expectations.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with emerging labor market weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 20 trading days following UNRATE increase > 0.4pp, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1 23400.294) combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) signals demand deceleration despite historical growth levels. This divergence indicates recession risk, prompting the Fed to cut rates and Treasury yields to fall. The interaction of slowing demand with elevated rates creates a policy pivot signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% over 3 months while GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging demand weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 60 trading days despite RSAFS decline > 2% and GDPC1 growth < 2%, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines below 101.0 signal supply-side contraction. Combined with persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 315), this stagflationary signal creates policy uncertainty. The Fed faces a dilemma between fighting inflation and supporting growth, causing Treasury yield volatility and downward pressure as recession fears dominate near-term pricing.
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with supply shock and persistent inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over 20 trading days when both INDPRO < 101.0 and CPIAUCSL > 315, or stagflation signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.
